user_query:
I’m organizing a tiny, evidence-based “cosmology mini fact-check” for my sci‑fi book club and I need a reproducible way to validate sources and tidy up my outreach. Please use the included inputs and produce all requested outputs under the specified paths.

Inputs (provided in the workspace):
- input/claims.csv — the set of claims to validate
- input/invite_draft.md — my rough outreach email to a local astronomy club
- input/allowed_domains.txt — patterns for domains considered credible
- input/email_requirements.yaml — constraints for rewriting and drafting messages

What to do:
1) Fact-check data gathering with live search:
   - For each claim in input/claims.csv, perform at least two distinct search engine queries to locate at least two distinct sources from credible organizations or peer‑reviewed publications relevant to the claim’s topic. Record the queries and sources you actually used.
   - Save all queries to logs/search_queries.csv with columns: claim_id,query. Include at least 2 queries per claim.
   - Save sources to data/factcheck_sources.jsonl, one JSON object per line, with fields: claim_id (string), query_used (string), source_title (string), source_domain (string, e.g., nasa.gov), url (string), publisher (string), accessed_utc (ISO 8601), rationale (string, 30–400 chars explaining why this source is relevant/credible for the claim).
   - Note: Use live Internet searches; do not fabricate sources. Multiple claims may share a source only if the rationale differs and is relevant to each.

2) Rewrite and draft outreach:
   - Rewrite input/invite_draft.md to be concise and friendly‑nerdy per input/email_requirements.yaml. The rewritten email must:
     • Start with a single line beginning with "Subject: " and include the subject_prefix from the YAML.
     • Be within the specified max_word_counts.invite limit.
     • Include all required_phrases from the YAML and a one‑sentence call‑to‑action mentioning the approximate time commitment noted in the YAML.
     • Save to outreach/invite_email.md.
   - Draft a short moderator DM asking permission to post a call for volunteers in a space/science community. It must:
     • Fit within max_word_counts.mod_dm.
     • Include all dm_required_keywords from the YAML (exact words, any order).
     • Be polite, neutral, and specific about the ask.
     • Save to outreach/mod_dm.txt.

3) Summary report:
   - Create reports/summary.md with one section per claim in input/claims.csv. For each claim, include exactly these three lines:
     • "Claim {ID}: {claim text}"
     • "Domains: {comma‑separated list of unique source_domain values you found for this claim}"
     • "Credible: Yes" if at least one source_domain for that claim matches any pattern in input/allowed_domains.txt (substring or suffix match is acceptable), otherwise "Credible: No".

4) Local validation script (test writing and validation):
   - Write tests/validate_outputs.py that, when run from the repo root, validates all deliverables without any network access. It must:
     • Load input/claims.csv, input/allowed_domains.txt, input/email_requirements.yaml.
     • Check logs/search_queries.csv exists, has headers claim_id,query, and has ≥2 queries per claim ID.
     • Check data/factcheck_sources.jsonl exists and, for every claim_id in input/claims.csv, has ≥2 entries with non‑empty fields; url starts with http; accessed_utc parses as ISO 8601; rationale length is between 30 and 400 characters.
     • Compute credibility per claim (as defined in step 3) and verify reports/summary.md includes the three required lines per claim with a Credible value that matches the computed result.
     • Validate outreach/invite_email.md and outreach/mod_dm.txt against input/email_requirements.yaml:
       - invite_email.md: first line starts with "Subject: " and contains the YAML subject_prefix; total word count ≤ max_word_counts.invite; contains every phrase in required_phrases (case‑insensitive substring check).
       - mod_dm.txt: total word count ≤ max_word_counts.mod_dm; contains all dm_required_keywords (case‑insensitive word-level check).
     • Write a machine‑readable report to reports/validation_report.json with keys: {"queries_ok": bool, "sources_ok": bool, "summary_ok": bool, "invite_ok": bool, "dm_ok": bool, "credible_counts": {claim_id: {"total": int, "credible": bool}}} and exit with code 0 if all checks pass, otherwise non‑zero.

Deliverables to create:
- logs/search_queries.csv
- data/factcheck_sources.jsonl
- outreach/invite_email.md
- outreach/mod_dm.txt
- reports/summary.md
- tests/validate_outputs.py
- reports/validation_report.json (written by the validator script)

Please do not hardcode external facts in the validator; it must only check structure, counts, formatting, and domain‑pattern credibility per input/allowed_domains.txt. Internet search is required to populate sources and queries; everything else must be locally verifiable from the workspace artifacts. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
